summary: Descriptive overview of proximatecausation in substantive law: foreseeability, intervening causes, scope of liability.
key_points: foreseeability is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for proximatecausation are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine proximatecausation in litigation.
The doctrine of proximatecausation has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding proximatecausation requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
